title: Neochilenia calderana
intro: Neochilenia calderana is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as a type of wild mustard or cress. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Neochilenia calderana include glucosinolates, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Neochilenia calderana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their expectorant and mucolytic properties.
